sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew, presented with a complaint of right knee pain after playing basketball, stating that he felt a pop in his knee, which started swelling and he was unable to walk.
He describes the pain as achy and rates it a 4 out of 10.
He has been taking Tylenol, which provided some relief.
He also mentions that the swelling has decreased since the previous night.
He denies taking any other pain medication.
He reports a history of hypertension and diabetes, taking Norvasc 5mg daily and metformin 500mg daily, but sometimes skips metformin due to stomach upset.
His blood sugar readings are usually around 150 when taken.
He also has a history of hypertension, with a current blood pressure of 140/80.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes.
He takes Norvasc 5mg daily and metformin 500mg daily.
He has a history of a grade 2 systolic ejection murmur.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Norvasc 5mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has pain to palpation of the medial aspect of the right knee, with some edema, swelling, and ecchymosis.
The varus and valgus test is negative.
The knee examination shows some swelling and redness.
Test Results: The patient's x-ray of the right knee was normal, showing no fractures or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a right MCL strain, which may be a tear, confirmed by an MRI pending.
Problem list: Hypertension; diabetes; MCL strain; Grade 2 systolic ejection murmur; past.
Plan:
I prescribed Tylenol as needed for pain.
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg for pain and swelling.
I ordered a knee immobilizer for the patient to wear for the next couple of weeks.
I ordered an MRI to confirm the MCL strain.
I increased the patient's Norvasc 5mg daily for hypertension.
I ordered a consult to nutrition to help improve the patient's diet.
I advised the patient to start taking their blood sugars daily and to bring the results back to track their progress.
Follow-up: The patient will return in two weeks for follow-up.
